question: How to decompose E-001 (dispense slice) into lisa work?

options.label: 2 stories / 8 tickets, scaffold-first DAG
options.rationale: keystone-first; parallelize seam/budget/log/baml after the scaffold

choice: 2 stories, 8 tickets; T-001-01 gates a parallel wave-1; T-002-03 the convergence; critical path 5
rationale: file-overlap modeled so wave 1 runs concurrently without conflict

outcome.verified: typecheck clean; 114/114 tests; live dispense A1 reproduced the same plan near-identically

question: Implementation stack for Vend?

options.label: TypeScript on Bun
options.rationale: typed + native Claude tooling, fast local

options.label: Rust
options.rationale: single binary, but claude only via subprocess

choice: TypeScript on Bun; BAML authoring-only (render+parse); claude -p transport
rationale: only option both typed and natively integrated; BAML role de-risked via mc-design-eval

outcome.verified: BAML-on-Bun smoke passed; build green; live A1 round-tripped b.request->claude -p->b.parse first try

question: Frame v0 as a closed measured loop (intent->plan->execute->measure->refine)?

options.label: teach an agent to compose tickets via BAML; defer the loop

choice: one BAML compose function; defer measurement/loop
humanCorrection: 'it's simpler than that' — stop elaborating past the slice; BAML is the node primitive

andon.trigger: elaborated v0 into a full closed measured loop
andon.fix: shrink to one BAML compose function

outcome.verified: the simpler framing is what built and ran

question: What is the first epic, scoped to the charter + codebase at the moment?
choice: E-001 dispense slice; CI deferred (blocked on scaffold)
rationale: pull grounded in the empty codebase surfaced CI's own dependency — the pull principle prioritized for us

question: Did lisa's 'done' tickets actually commit their code?
choice: FLAGGED — 8 tickets 'done' but only T-002-03 committed; scaffold/modules/baml/work-artifacts sit uncommitted in the working tree
rationale: lisa advances phase on artifact detection, not on commit

andon.trigger: lisa 'done' != 'committed'; RDSPI Implement's 'commit incrementally' did not hold across the parallel sessions
andon.fix: commit the working tree now; consider a gate that fails a ticket whose code is uncommitted

question: What drives DecomposeEpic's token cost / the budget fat-tail?
choice: FINDING — claude -p is the full agent; A2's tiny fixture burned 119k tokens > A1's full E-001 (78k); the fat tail is agentic wandering, not input size
note: first budget-calibration data point per demand.md; highest-value follow-up = cross-board id-collision guard before pointing the play at a populated board (A1 only stayed safe by materializing into a sandbox)

question: After E-001 converged, what is the next wave of stories and the recommended next pull across E-002 CI / E-003 shelf / E-004 id-guard / E-005 model-id / the casting-engine sorcery?

options.label: E-004 id-guard next, then E-005, then escalate the E-003-shelf vs E-002-CI fork, then E-007 sorcery
options.rationale: leverage x readiness: E-004 is the cheap ready enabler that unblocks machine-decomposing every later epic in place; the shelf-vs-CI trade is a genuine fork demand.md refuses to resolve

options.label: Pull E-003 shelf immediately (core feature first)
options.rationale: advances P2 directly, but unsafe to dispense in place until E-004 lands

options.label: Pull E-002 CI first (de-risk the fleet)
options.rationale: enabler; weakest check type by our own lens, de-risks work that mostly doesn't exist yet

choice: Ranked spine E-004 -> E-005 -> [fork: E-003 shelf (recommended) vs E-002 CI (alternative)] -> E-007 sorcery; epic-scoped story ids to dodge the flat-scheme collision (F1)
rationale: applies demand.md's value+budget instrument verbatim; escalates the real fork per project-steering.md instead of fabricating a winner; resists over-build by planning one wave only
note: E-006 sorcery cast. Bounds check found F1 (story-id scheme self-contradiction), F2 (demand.md internally stale on E-001 blocked-rows), F3 (E-002 has no epic card), F4 (RDSPI-vs-sorcery friction, in review.md). Principle layer coherent.

question: Resolve the E-003-shelf vs E-002-CI fork (D-007); sweep the board now that the shelf shipped.

options.label: E-003 shelf
options.rationale: core-feature counter (P2); E-004 unlocks safe in-place dispense

choice: Pulled E-003 (the recommended side); E-003/E-004/E-005 converged; board swept to mark them done so the menu renders truth
rationale: the human resolved D-007 by pulling the shelf

outcome.verified: typecheck clean; 229 tests; bare `vend` renders a ranked menu

note: Two findings: (1) commit-discipline andon D-005 did NOT recur across E-004/E-005 and E-003 — two consecutive loops self-committed incrementally; D-005 trending resolved. (2) the shelf surfaced demand.md staleness (it rendered done E-003 as 'ready'), which prompted this sweep — the counter shows board truth, so a stale board is now visible.

question: E-002 CI backstop done — is the v0 set complete, and what is the frontier?
choice: v0 complete: E-001 lever, E-003 shelf, E-004 id-guard, E-005 model-id, E-002 CI backstop all done; main demand signals cleared; board swept; vend shows nothing ready
rationale: the jidoka inspection layer (tps.md/go-and-see) now exists; the frontier shifts from pulling epics to building the specs into BAML plays

outcome.verified: independently ran `dagger -m ci call test run` — bun test in-container, 7.6s warm, drift-free; app 229 tests green; /ci imports nothing from app; engine pinned v0.21.4

note: Three findings: (1) commit-discipline D-005 resolved — third consecutive loop self-committed incrementally. (2) keep-warm now empirically motivated (cold-start ~18.4s vs warm ~0.2s connect) — the next CI signal, recorded out-of-slice not built. (3) the clearing pipeline is fully spec'd FROM DEMONSTRATIONS — DecomposeEpic R1-R12, ProposeEpic PE-1-PE-7, decomposition-records — so the next move is making the specs real (E-007 casting-engine / the DecomposeEpic build), crossing from hand-cleared to machine-cleared.

question: Is E-007's casting-engine first slice real — does the engine cast >=2 plays — and did the loop commit cleanly?
choice: VERIFIED: the engine casts >=2 plays through one play-agnostic castPlay; BUT the D-005 commit andon RECURRED — the loop left HEAD inconsistent and the residual was hand-committed
rationale: independently cast CaptureNote live and inspected git

andon.trigger: the E-007 loop committed only T-007-02, leaving T-007-01/03/04 source uncommitted AND committed cast.ts without its play.ts dep — HEAD did not build; D-005 regressed after 3 clean loops
andon.fix: hand-committed the residual (0e1ffcf) to restore a green HEAD; durable fix is a CI/hook gate that fails a ticket whose code is uncommitted — E-002's backstop is the home

outcome.verified: live CaptureNote cast: outcome=success, wrote its note, run-log $0.115; 266 tests green; castPlay has no per-play branches (the DecomposeEpic refs are comments)

note: Findings: (1) D-005 correlates with cross-file churn — it held for 3 small/low-overlap loops, broke on E-007's 3 tickets all refactoring the shared decompose-epic.ts/cli.ts/press.ts. (2) keystone met (agnostic castPlay, >=2 plays, a sorcery casts not-via-RDSPI = partial F4) but input-assembly + `vend run <any-play>` stay DecomposeEpic-shaped — full generalization is a follow-up. (3) mana economics confirmed live: the Red sorcery cast for $0.12 vs DecomposeEpic's ~$0.44-0.74.

question: Does E-008's uncommitted-code gate actually fix D-005?
choice: VERIFIED + FIXED: check:committed fails on dirty source (names the file, exit 1) and passes clean; the on-stop hook blocks the stop (exit 2) on uncommitted source and fails open. D-005 closed.
rationale: dogfooded the gate both ways (verified the verifier) and inspected the hook

outcome.verified: dirty src/zz-drift-test.ts -> check:committed exit 1 naming the file; rm -> exit 0; 282 tests green; hook blocks stop (exit 2) + fails open

note: The bootstrap closed: the gate's own loop self-committed ALL its SOURCE cleanly (4 commits, unlike E-007) — plausibly on-stop.sh enforcing once committed mid-build (the fix gating its own construction). Honest boundaries: (1) the gate scopes to SOURCE (src/baml_src/ci); doc/bookkeeping residual (ticket flips, work artifacts) is still swept by steering — low-risk, not build-breaking. (2) it catches uncommitted source, not an inconsistent-but-committed HEAD (the E-007 cast.ts-without-play.ts case) — that is the follow-up container-side HEAD-builds gate.
